title: The marine dinoflagellates Heterocapsa horiguchii and Gambierdiscus polynesiensis isolated from Norfolk Island
author: Rhodes, Lesley
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: An expedition to Norfolk Island in 2025, led by the Australian Museum, was carried out to describe the biodiversity there. Investigation of samples collected from macroalgae resulted in the isolation of dinoflagellates in the genera    Heterocapsa  and  Gambierdiscus . Both are known to include species which produce toxins. DNA sequencing results showed that the  Heterocapsa  isolate was  H. horiguchii  but it was also shown to be non-toxic to mice by intraperitoneal injection. The  Gambierdiscus  isolate was  G. polynesiensis  (DNA sequencing)   and produced 44-methylgambierone, gambierone and an as yet unknown compound.

Both are known to include species which produce toxins. DNA sequencing results showed that the  Heterocapsa  isolate was  H. horiguchii  but it was also shown to be non-toxic to mice by intraperitoneal injection. The  Gambierdiscus  isolate was  G. polynesiensis  (DNA sequencing)   and produced 44-methylgambierone, gambierone and an as yet unknown compound.
